content.problemStatement:
### **Aptitude Practice Question**

A boy is facing south and then he turns 60° in the clockwise direction and then turns to 105° in the anticlockwise direction. In which direction now he is facing?

---

### **Answer & Explanation**

The boy faces in the direction of AB. On moving 60° in the clockwise direction, he faces in the direction AC. On further moving 105° in the anticlockwise direction, he faces in the direction AD. Hence the boy is finally facing in a southeast direction.Question 4: If the South becomes northwest, the East becomes southwest, and so on, then what will the North become?Answer: Directions are changing by 135° in a clockwise direction. So the answer will be southeast as 90°+ 45° = 135° then, 90° of the north (Right of the north is east) is east and then 45° of the east (45° Right of the east is southeast) is southeast. Also Check:➣ Test your knowledge- Quiz!
